title: Firestore MCP + event-sourcing tools + replay helper (trip-planner Round 3)
description: Add `repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/firestore.yaml` — a new general-purpose MCP playbook for Firestore Native mode CRUD plus event-sourcing primitives (append-only event log with monotonic seq and header redaction). Plus `ai-meta/scripts/firestore_replay.sh` — a helper that walks a thread's event log for offline inspection and (later, Round 4) agent diff. Round 3 of the trip-planner project (Adiona/muno). Independent of the agent itself — Round 4 consumes these primitives. Cap: 1 ops PR (new MCP playbook + tools/list catalog) + 1 ai-meta change for the replay helper + 1 docs PR. User-action pre-handoff DONE: Firestore Native mode db `(default)` in us-central1 exists; worker SA has `roles/datastore.user`. Scoping reference: `sync/issues/2026-05-12-trip-planner-app-scoping.md` (sections 'Event-sourced storage + replay' and 'Firestore data model').

pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.status: DONE 2026-05-12. Verified by claude-cowork before handoff.
pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.what_was_done: 1) `gcloud services enable firestore.googleapis.com` on `noetl-demo-19700101`. 2) `gcloud firestore databases create --location=us-central1 --type=firestore-native --project=noetl-demo-19700101` — created `(default)` database in Native mode, region locked to `us-central1`. 3) `gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding ... --member=serviceAccount:noetl-worker-mcp@noetl-demo-19700101.iam.gserviceaccount.com --role=roles/datastore.user`. Phase 1 re-verifies all three.
pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.no_new_secrets: Firestore auth uses Workload Identity on `noetl-worker-mcp` (same pattern Google Places + Vertex AI use). No Secret Manager involvement.

design_brief_for_codex.playbook_shape:
`repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/firestore.yaml` mirrors the shape of `mcp/google-places.yaml` (Workload Identity, no token secret). Workload fields:
```yaml
workload:
  gcp_auth: google_oauth
  gcp_project: noetl-demo-19700101
  firestore_database: "(default)"
  firestore_api_base: https://firestore.googleapis.com/v1
  method: tools/list
  tool: ""
  arguments: {}
```
Auth header on every Firestore REST call: `Authorization: Bearer <SA OAuth token>` (refreshed via `google.auth.default(scopes=['https://www.googleapis.com/auth/datastore'])`). Use the REST surface at `projects/{project}/databases/(default)/documents/...`. Reference: https://firestore.googleapis.com/$discovery/rest?version=v1.
design_brief_for_codex.tool_surface:
Six tools, mirroring the catalog shape of the existing Duffel / Google Places playbooks:

  1. **`set_doc`** — write or upsert. Inputs: `path` (e.g., `chat_threads/abc123`), `data` (dict), optional `merge: bool` (default false — replace). Calls `PATCH /projects/{proj}/databases/{db}/documents/{path}` with `documentMask.fieldPaths` (when merge=true). Returns the persisted doc.

  2. **`get_doc`** — read. Inputs: `path`. Calls `GET /projects/{proj}/databases/{db}/documents/{path}`. Returns the doc or `status: not_found`.

  3. **`query_collection`** — query. Inputs: `collection_path`, optional `where` (array of `{field, op, value}` clauses; ops: `=`, `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`, `in`, `array-contains`), optional `order_by` (`{field, direction: asc|desc}`), optional `limit` (default 50, max 500). Uses `runQuery` with a structured query. Returns matching docs.

  4. **`delete_doc`** — delete. Inputs: `path`. Calls `DELETE /projects/{proj}/databases/{db}/documents/{path}`. Used mainly for test fixture teardown.

  5. **`append_event`** — append-only insert into a `{thread_path}/events` subcollection with a monotonic `seq`. Inputs: `thread_path` (e.g., `chat_threads/abc123`), `event` (dict: `{type, payload, parent_seq?}`). Behaviour: open a Firestore transaction → read the highest existing `seq` in `{thread_path}/events` → assign `seq = max + 1` → REDACT known sensitive header keys in the event payload before write (see 'Header redaction rules' below) → write to `{thread_path}/events/<auto_id>` with `seq`, `server_timestamp`, `type`, redacted `payload`. Returns the new event's path + seq. Transactional so concurrent appends don't collide on seq.

  6. **`replay_events`** — read an event range. Inputs: `thread_path`, optional `seq_from` (default 0), `seq_to` (default infinity), optional `type_filter` (array of event type strings). Calls a structured query over `{thread_path}/events`, ordered by `seq` ascending. Returns the events array. No agent-diff in this round — that integration is Round 4's job. This tool just exposes the event stream for offline inspection.
design_brief_for_codex.header_redaction_rules:
In `append_event`, scan the event's `payload` recursively for dicts. For any dict key matching the following case-insensitive set, replace the value with the literal string `<REDACTED>`:
  - `authorization`
  - `x-goog-user-project`
  - `x-goog-api-key`
  - `x-figma-token`
  - `x-api-key`
  - `cookie`
  - `set-cookie`
  - any key matching the regex `(?i).*(token|secret|password|api[-_]?key|client[-_]?secret).*` whose value is a string. NEVER recurse into the redacted value (don't try to parse JWT internals etc.).
The redaction runs ONLY in `append_event`. `set_doc` writes the data verbatim — caller is responsible for whatever they store there. (We redact at the event-log layer because event logs are the audit trail; arbitrary doc writes might legitimately store hashed credentials etc.)
Unit-test the redaction logic by appending an event with `payload.request.headers.Authorization = 'Bearer abc123'`, reading back via `replay_events`, and asserting the value is `<REDACTED>`.
design_brief_for_codex.tools_list_catalog_shape: Per the MCP spec — each entry: `{name, description, inputSchema: <JSON Schema>}`. Use `mcp/google-places.yaml`'s tools/list step as the canonical example. The six tools above plus get_token-style debug entries are NOT needed (no token to fetch — WI is implicit).
design_brief_for_codex.scripts_firestore_replay_sh:
`ai-meta/scripts/firestore_replay.sh` — a small Bash + Python helper analogous to `figma_fetch.sh`. Reads via the Firestore REST API using the operator's local gcloud creds (NOT the worker SA — this is a debugging tool). Usage:
```
firestore_replay.sh events <thread_path> [--from N] [--to N] [--type-filter type1,type2]
firestore_replay.sh thread-list [--limit N]
firestore_replay.sh doc <doc_path>
```
No agent integration in this round — the script just prints the event stream and structured doc reads. Round 4's first deliverable extends this with a `replay --against-agent` mode that re-feeds events and diffs the agent's emissions.
The script uses `gcloud auth print-access-token` for auth (operator's local context), pipes JSON through `python3 -m json.tool` for readability, never echoes credentials. Add an entry to ai-meta's CLAUDE.md or a new `playbooks/firestore-replay.md` (decide which is more discoverable) referencing the script.
design_brief_for_codex.no_data_model_assumption: The MCP tools are GENERIC — they don't bake in the trip-planner-specific `chat_threads/{threadId}/events` hierarchy. The data model documented in the scoping doc is a CONVENTION the trip-planner agent (Round 4) follows; the Firestore tools just CRUD whatever paths the caller hands them. This keeps the playbook reusable for any future agent.

1_verify_pre_handoff.objective: Re-verify what claude-cowork already confirmed before handoff.
spec: `gcloud services list --enabled --project=noetl-demo-19700101 --filter='name:firestore.googleapis.com'` — must return the service.
`gcloud firestore databases describe --database='(default)' --project=noetl-demo-19700101 --format='value(type,locationId)'` — must show `FIRESTORE_NATIVE` and `us-central1`.

`gcloud projects get-iam-policy noetl-demo-19700101 --flatten='bindings[].members' --filter='bindings.members:noetl-worker-mcp@noetl-demo-19700101.iam.gserviceaccount.com AND bindings.role:roles/datastore.user'` — must return the role.

Worker pod probe: `kubectl -n noetl exec deploy/noetl-worker -- python -c 'import google.auth, urllib.request, urllib.error; c,_=google.auth.default(scopes=["https://www.googleapis.com/auth/datastore"]); import google.auth.transport.requests as t; c.refresh(t.Request()); r=urllib.request.Request("https://firestore.googleapis.com/v1/projects/noetl-demo-19700101/databases/(default)", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + c.token}); print(urllib.request.urlopen(r,timeout=10).status)'` — must print 200 (database accessible from WI).
If any check fails: AMBER + STOP.
Create the playbook file per `design_brief_for_codex.playbook_shape` and `tool_surface`.

Each tool's HTTP step uses the Firestore REST API. JSON encoding: Firestore REST wraps values in type-tagged dicts (`{stringValue: 'x'}`, `{integerValue: '42'}`, etc.). Write a Python helper inside the playbook to convert plain dicts → Firestore-typed dicts on write, and Firestore-typed → plain on read. Don't reinvent — Firestore's official `google.cloud.firestore` library does this, but to avoid pulling new deps prefer a minimal hand-rolled encoder/decoder.

`append_event` MUST use a Firestore transaction (POST .../databases/(default):beginTransaction → query for max seq → write → commit). The transaction guarantees no two concurrent appends collide on `seq`.

Implement header redaction per `design_brief_for_codex.header_redaction_rules`. Reject any attempt to disable it via an `arguments.skip_redaction` flag — there is no such flag; the redaction is always applied in `append_event`.
Update tools/list to list all 6 tools.
Open a PR against repos/ops main: `feat(mcp): add Firestore MCP playbook with event-sourcing primitives`.

3_direct_mcp_smokes.objective: Verify each tool works against the live Firestore (default) database from inside a worker pod.
spec: Re-register the playbook on GKE.
Smoke A — tools/list: invoke method=tools/list. Expect 6 tools.

Smoke B — set_doc + get_doc round-trip: write a synthetic doc at `_smoke/round3-firestore-{nonce}` with payload `{a: 1, b: 'hello', nested: {c: [1,2,3]}}`. Read it back. Assert deep equality after Firestore-typed encode/decode.

Smoke C — query_collection: write 3 docs at `_smoke/round3-firestore-{nonce}/items/{1,2,3}` with `{score: 1|5|3}`. Query with `where: [{field: score, op: >=, value: 3}]`, `order_by: {field: score, direction: desc}`, `limit: 10`. Expect 2 docs in score order [5, 3].

Smoke D — append_event monotonic seq: append 5 events to `_smoke/round3-firestore-{nonce}/events` with type `test`. Read back via replay_events. Assert seq values are [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] in order regardless of write order.

Smoke E — header redaction: append an event with payload `{request: {headers: {Authorization: 'Bearer abc123xyz', Accept: 'application/json'}}, response: {status: 200}}`. Read back via replay_events. Assert `payload.request.headers.Authorization == '<REDACTED>'` and `payload.request.headers.Accept == 'application/json'` (untouched).

Smoke F — replay_events type filter: append events of types `chat`, `tool_call`, `tool_response` to `_smoke/round3-firestore-{nonce}/events`. Replay with `type_filter: ['tool_call']`. Assert only tool_call events return.
Smoke G — delete_doc cleanup: delete the entire `_smoke/round3-firestore-{nonce}` subtree (delete docs one by one if no recursive delete). Verify get_doc returns not_found.
Capture all execution IDs for the result file.
3_direct_mcp_smokes.verify: All seven smokes complete; transactional seq holds under concurrent writes (if practical to test); redaction holds; type filter works.

4_scripts_firestore_replay_sh.objective: Add the replay helper to ai-meta.
spec: Create `ai-meta/scripts/firestore_replay.sh` per `design_brief_for_codex.scripts_firestore_replay_sh`. Uses `gcloud auth print-access-token` for the operator's local context.
Supports: `events <thread_path> [--from N] [--to N] [--type-filter csv]`, `thread-list [--limit N]`, `doc <doc_path>`.
Smoke the script locally against the `_smoke/round3-firestore-{nonce}` fixture before tearing it down in phase 3 smoke G. Capture an example output in the result JSON (truncated, no sensitive data).
Add a brief mention in ai-meta/CLAUDE.md under 'Quick commands (manual)' so operators discover it.
4_scripts_firestore_replay_sh.verify: Script runs from any user with project-read access; output is readable JSON.

spec: Edit `repos/docs/docs/tutorials/07-travel-agent-with-widgets.md` — add a short subsection 'Event-sourced storage (Round 3)' noting that mcp/firestore is now available, the 6-tool surface, and that the trip-planner agent (Round 4) will use it for chat thread + trip event persistence. ~150 words.
Open docs PR: `docs(tutorials): note mcp/firestore + event-sourcing primitives`.

6_close_out.objective: Write result JSON, append codex-spike validation log, write memory entry.
spec: Result file: `bridge/outbox/20260512-235000-firestore-mcp-event-sourcing.result.json`. Shape mirrors round 20260512-220000.
Append a paragraph to `bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md`.

Memory entry: `ai-meta/memory/inbox/2026/05/<timestamp>-firestore-mcp-event-sourcing-green.md` (muno doesn't exist yet — Round 6 bootstraps it; for now trip-planner outcome memory lands in ai-meta and will be referenced from muno once it exists).
Verify the `_smoke/...` collection was deleted (no test data left in production Firestore).
6_close_out.verify: Result + memory + validation log all in place; production Firestore has zero `_smoke/...` collections.

architectural_rules: Workload Identity auth only. No token secret, no service account key file.
MCP tools are GENERIC. Don't bake in the trip-planner-specific data model (chat_threads, trips, events under threads, etc.) — the agent (Round 4) provides the convention.
Header redaction is mandatory in append_event. No skip_redaction flag.
Smokes write to `_smoke/round3-firestore-{nonce}` paths. ALL smoke data is deleted at end of phase 3 (smoke G). Production Firestore is left empty.
Hand-roll the Firestore-typed JSON encoder/decoder; don't introduce a new Python dep (google-cloud-firestore) on the worker image without separate approval.
Transactional seq assignment in append_event — no eventual-consistency hacks.
No release cut. No git push from ai-meta.
If pre-handoff verification fails (any of the 4 checks): AMBER + STOP.
result_file_instructions: Write `bridge/outbox/20260512-235000-firestore-mcp-event-sourcing.result.json`. Top-level: {id, status, summary, pr_summary, gke_registration {mcp_firestore_version, catalog_id}, pre_handoff_checks, smokes: {tools_list, set_get_roundtrip, query_collection, append_event_seq, header_redaction, replay_type_filter, delete_cleanup}, replay_script_smoke, cleanup {smoke_collections_deleted}, cleanup, verdict}. Mirror the round 20260512-220000 result shape. Never log full Firestore document data beyond what's needed to prove the assertion (truncate >200 chars).
references: sync/issues/2026-05-12-trip-planner-app-scoping.md — full trip-planner scoping (Event-sourced storage + replay + Firestore data model sections)
repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/google-places.yaml — WI-auth playbook pattern to mirror (no token secret)
memory/inbox/2026/05/20260512-230000-duffel-stays-unavailable-round-2.md — Round 2 outcome (Stays unavailable, Round 4 uses Amadeus hotels)
